source_record.title: Mechanisms for resistance to BCMA-targeted immunotherapies in multiple myeloma.
source_record.study_context: Review article in Blood Reviews covering BCMA-targeted immunotherapies in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, including CAR-T, bispecific T-cell engagers, and antibody-drug conjugates; PubMed EFetch abstract and metadata used for this v0 public extraction.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The review attributes part of BCMA-targeted immunotherapy resistance to T cell-driven factors, making T-cell function context a source-level mechanism category to track for CAR-T and T-cell engager resistance mapping.
limitations: Review-level abstract statement; it is not a cohort-level measurement.
The public abstract does not specify exhaustion markers, persistence duration, expansion kinetics, or product-level comparisons.
This signal supports extraction-field coverage, not a frequency estimate or patient-specific relapse explanation.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The review lists the immune tumor microenvironment as a resistance-mechanism category, so public CAR-T fitness extraction should preserve immune-context fields that may shape T-cell activity and durability.
limitations: The abstract does not provide source-specific immune-cell subsets, biomarkers, or assay methods.
Microenvironment category framing should not be treated as proof that a given patient's relapse is immune-context driven.
Requires full-text and source-by-source extraction before mechanistic comparison.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The review identifies antigen-dependency as one of the main attributed categories for resistance to BCMA-targeted immunotherapies.
limitations: Review-level category only; the abstract does not provide target-status numerator, denominator, assay, or timing fields.
Does not estimate antigen-loss or antigen-low frequency.
Does not support product comparison or treatment sequencing.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The review states that resistance understanding remains uncertain and organizes mechanisms into broad categories, making review-level framing distinct from quantitative source-specific measurement facts.
measurement_fields: cohort measurement not provided in abstract
limitations: Do not convert broad review categories into cohort-level measurements.
Do not compare categories as evidence strength or biological priority from this extraction.
Long-term survival impact and resistance mechanisms remain framed as uncertain in the source abstract.

what_this_supports: CAR T fitness, T-cell-driven factors, and immune tumor microenvironment context now have a second public source-specific extraction record.
Review-level BCMA resistance categories should be tracked separately from cohort-level measurements.
Antigen-dependency, T-cell-driven factors, and immune tumor microenvironment categories can guide extraction fields for later full-text or primary-study records.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not prove that CAR T exhaustion, persistence loss, or immune microenvironment effects are dominant relapse mechanisms.
It does not estimate mechanism frequency across BCMA-targeted CAR-T, TCE, or ADC products.
It does not compare therapies, recommend therapy sequencing, or establish clinical efficacy for resistance-overcoming strategies.
It does not provide patient-specific diagnosis, prognosis, treatment, trial, or cure guidance.

review_notes: Built from NCBI EFetch PubMed XML and ESummary metadata for PMID 39818472.
No PMC full-text record was available through the NCBI ID converter at extraction time, so this v0 extraction is limited to PubMed abstract and metadata.
Created to complete public issue #15 as second-source coverage for CAR T fitness, exhaustion, expansion, or persistence.
The public extraction surface is stewarded by frg.earth; scientific claims remain source-scoped to the cited public records.
